sanskrit: एवं बुद्धेः परं बुद्ध्वा संस्तभ्यात्मानमात्मना । जहि शत्रुं महाबाहो कामरूपं दुरासदम् ॥
hindiTranslation: इन्द्रियोंको स्थूलशरीरसे पर श्रेष्ठ, सबल, प्रकाशक, व्यापक तथा सूक्ष्म कहते हैं ।  इन्द्रियोंसे पर मन है, मनसे भी पर बुद्धि है औऱ जो बुद्धिसे भी पर है वह काम है ।  इस तरह बुद्धिसे पर  काम को जानकर अपने द्वारा अपनेआपको वशमें करके हे महाबाहो  तू इस कामरूप दुर्जय शत्रुको मार डाल ॥
englishTranslation: Thus, knowing the higher beyond the intellect, steady the lower self by the higher self, and destroy that enemy in the form of desire, which is hard to conquer.
essence: Desire loses power when the higher mind takes command.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Recognize that desire is not the highest force in you. Use your deeper discernment to steady yourself and overcome it.
simpleInsight: You are not helpless before desire; a steadier part of you can lead.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna is still frozen. Krishna has just mapped the chain from senses to mind to intellect, and now names the real enemy: desire. The teaching turns inward because the war begins inside.
modernRelevance: You open the app to check one message and end up scrolling for an hour. The urge feels automatic, but another part of you can still take the wheel.
translationLiteral: एवम् (thus) / बुद्धेः (of the intellect) / परम् (higher) / बुद्ध्वा (knowing) / संस्तभ्य (steadying) / आत्मानम् (the lower self) / आत्मना (by the higher self) / जहि (destroy) / शत्रुम् (enemy) / महाबाहो (mighty-armed one) / कामरूपम् (in the form of desire) / दुरासदम् (hard to conquer)
